§6Satellites§f

§aOrbital payloads for the Soyuz-FG — resource scanning, mapping, and exotic resource acquisition!§f

§bSatellites§f are orbital payloads launched via the §bSoyuz-FG§f rocket. Each satellite has a specific function and must be linked to a §bSatellite Control Interface§f (using the Satellite Laser Designator, hbm:sat_designator) before launch.

§6Available Satellite Types§f:
§b• Surface Mapping Satellite§f (hbm:sat_mapper) — maps the surface from orbit
§b• Orbital Death Ray§f (hbm:sat_laser) — precision laser strike capability
§b• Satellite Radar Relay§f (hbm:sat_relay) — communication relay
§b• Xenium Resonator Satellite§f (hbm:sat_resonator) — exotic resource detection
§b• Depth-Resource Scanner§f (hbm:sat_scanner) — underground resource scanning
§b• All other standard satellite functions§f — various utility roles

§6Key Requirement§f:
Each satellite needs a §bSatellite Control Interface§f set to the same frequency. Use the §bSatellite Laser Designator§f to apply a random code, then ensure the satellite matches before launch.

§cWarning§f: Triple-check your frequency matching BEFORE launching. An unlinked satellite will be lost forever, wasting its extremely expensive resources!

§6Gerald Connection§f:
Gerald The Construction Android also requires an §bOrbital Module§f launched on the Soyuz-FG. The orbital module is assembled in an assembly machine:
Recipe: Aluminium Shell×4 + Rubber Bar×16 + Solid Fuel×16 + Small Thruster×3 + Advanced Control Unit×3 + Low-Density Element

§eTip§f: Build a satellite control interface and designator before your first Soyuz launch. Practice with a cheaper satellite first before attempting expensive payloads like Xenium Resonator or the Orbital Module for Gerald.
